html:
[3239]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秘省挍書 - A title or office within the imperial secretariat, possibly a librarian or archivist.
  • 河東薛義 - A proper name, 'Xue Yi from Hedong.'
  • 妹夫 - Refers to a brother-in-law, specifically 'Feng Ji' in this context.
  • 桐庐尉 - A local official title, 'magistrate of Tonglu.'
  • 叔母 - Refers to a maternal aunt, 'Wang Shi' in this context.
  • 痁疾 - A type of malaria or intermittent fever.
  • 四山之神 - A reference to deities associated with four mountains, possibly a protective or local deity.
  • 六丁使者 - A term from Taoist mythology, referring to six divine messengers or generals.
  • 五道将军 - Another term from Taoist mythology, referring to five generals who control different paths or directions.
  • 精气 - Refers to vital energy or spirit.
  • 神魂 - Refers to the soul or spirit.
  • 勃疟 - A severe form of malaria or intermittent fever.
  • 律令 - Refers to legal or divine commandments.
  • 传呪 - To transmit or recite a spell or incantation.
  • 至心持之 - To hold or recite with sincere devotion.
  • 少女 - Refers to a young girl.
  • 蚝毛 - A term that is unclear, possibly a typo or archaic term; it may refer to a type of hair or fur.
  • 黑犬 - Refers to a black dog.
  • 可急擒杀之 - Can be captured and killed immediately.
  • 二小婢 - Refers to two young female servants.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秘省 - The imperial secretariat or library, a place of scholarly work and record-keeping.
  • 河东 - A historical region in Shanxi province, often associated with the Xue family.
  • 桐庐 - A county in Zhejiang province, indicating the location of Feng Ji's office.
  • 痁疾 - A specific type of intermittent fever, often associated with malaria.
  • 四山之神 - A reference to local or protective deities, possibly specific to the region.
  • 六丁使者 - A term from Taoist mythology, often used in spells and incantations.
  • 五道将军 - Another term from Taoist mythology, often used in spells and incantations.
  • 广异记 - A collection of strange tales and supernatural stories, indicating the source of this narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛义 - Xue Yi, the main character of the story.
  • 崔秘 - Feng Ji, Xue Yi's brother-in-law.
  • 桐庐 - Tonglu, the location of Feng Ji's office.
  • 韦氏 - Wang Shi, Xue Yi's maternal aunt.
  • 广异记 - A collection of strange tales and supernatural stories, the source of this narrative.

[3240]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夢自御牛車 - A dream about driving a carriage pulled by oxen, symbolizing a journey or progress.
  • 瑞日光燭天地 - A luminous sun that illuminates the entire world, often used to describe a significant or auspicious event.
  • 自執靷行通衢 - Holding the reins and walking along a main road, indicating a sense of control and direction.
  • 俄而驚寤 - Suddenly waking up from the dream, often used to describe a moment of realization or shock.
  • 竒叹 - Expressing surprise and admiration, often used to describe a reaction to something unusual or remarkable.
  • 累拜 - Repeatedly promoted or appointed to higher positions.
  • 果契前夢 - The prediction or dream comes true, indicating that the dream was accurate.
  • 近十舉而名未播 - Having been nominated for the imperial examinations many times but not yet gaining fame.
  • 似有人報云 - As if someone told him, suggesting a dream or a voice in his mind.
  • 宋秀才頭上戴山 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a difficult or challenging situation.
  • 無因成名 - No reason to become famous, indicating a lack of opportunity.
  • 去之自當通泰 - Removing the obstacle will lead to success, suggesting that removing a hindrance will bring good fortune.
  • 覺來便思去之不可名 - Upon waking, he thought it was impossible to remove the obstacle, indicating a sense of frustration.
  • 獄遂去二犬 - The name '岳' (mountain) was changed to '言' (speech), removing the two characters '山' and '犬' (dog).
  • 解首送也 - The top candidate in the examination, indicating a high achievement.
  • 京兆尹張毅夫 - The governor of the capital region, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 馮涯侍郎 - A junior official, indicating a lower-ranking official.
  • 解送舉人有私 - The official had a private relationship with the candidate, indicating a conflict of interest.
  • 退解頭為第六十五人 - The top candidate was demoted to the 65th position, indicating a significant setback.
  • 來春之事甘已參差 - The next year's events would be difficult, indicating a sense of resignation.
  • 第四人及第 - Ranking fourth in the imperial examinations, indicating a high achievement despite the setback.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 會昌六年春 - The year 846 AD, during the Tang Dynasty, when Emperor Wuzong was in power.
  • 宣宗即位 - The ascension of Emperor Xuanzong, who ruled from 846 to 859 AD.
  • 元舅之故 - Due to being the emperor's maternal uncle, indicating a close family relationship.
  • 大中十一年 - The year 857 AD, during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 府解 - The local examination, a preliminary step in the imperial examination system.
  • 試官 - The examiner, responsible for evaluating the candidates in the imperial examinations.
  • 解首 - The top candidate in the examination, indicating a high achievement.
  • 京兆尹張毅夫 - The governor of the capital region, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 澧州司戸 - A distant place, indicating a severe penalty.
  • 李潘舍人 - A high-ranking official, indicating a significant figure in the imperial examinations.

Proper Nouns

  • 鄭光 - A person's name.
  • 宣宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 宋言 - A person's name.
  • 馮涯 - A person's name.
  • 張毅夫 - A person's name.
  • 李潘 - A person's name.

[3241]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 判度支 - A high-ranking official position in the Tang Dynasty, equivalent to the modern-day Ministry of Finance.
  • 台辅之望 - Refers to the expectation of becoming a high-ranking official, similar to the Three Excellencies (三公).
  • 梦剃髪为僧 - A dream where one is shaved to become a monk, symbolizing a desire for spiritual renunciation.
  • 占梦多验 - The belief that dreams can be interpreted and often come true.
  • 登庸 - To be promoted to a high office.
  • 出家者号剃度 - The act of shaving one's head to become a monk is called '剃度.'
  • 大拜 - A significant promotion to a high-ranking position.
  • 微时 - Refers to a time of low status or humble beginnings.
  • 旌幢之贵 - A high rank or noble position, often associated with military or ceremonial honors.
  • 自普王即位 - Refers to the ascension of Emperor Xizong of Tang from the Prince of Pu.
  • 多能 - Skilled in many areas.
  • 不晓碁 - Does not know how to play Go, a traditional Chinese board game.
  • 梦人以碁经三卷焚而使吞之 - A dream where someone burns three volumes of a Go manual and makes the dreamer swallow them.
  • 待诏观碁 - Officials who were called upon to observe and advise on Go games.
  • 指画皆出人意 - Moves and strategies that surpass ordinary human thought.
  • 员外郎 - A junior official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 本名审义 - Originally named Shen Yi.
  • 忽梦一孝子引令上檀香树 - A sudden dream where a filial son leads the dreamer up a sandalwood tree.
  • 绯衣令服之 - A red robe is ordered to be worn.
  • 闲居力困 - Living in seclusion and feeling exhausted.
  • 遗刘新绯公服一领 - A new red official robe is given to Liu.
  • 征梦焉 - The dream comes true.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曹确 - A high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杜相 - Refers to Du Xunhe, a high-ranking official who was sent to govern Jiangxi.
  • 刘仁恭 - A historical figure who rose to power in the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 僖宗 - Emperor Xizong of Tang, who ascended the throne as the Prince of Pu.
  • 王蜀 - Refers to the state of Shu during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 刘檀 - A historical figure who rose to prominence in the state of Shu.
  • 北梦琐言 - A historical text that records various anecdotes and stories from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 玉谿编事 - A historical text that records various anecdotes and stories from the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 曹确 - Cao Que
  • 杜相 - Du Xunhe
  • 刘仁恭 - Liu Rengong
  • 僖宗 - Emperor Xizong of Tang
  • 刘檀 - Liu Tan
  • 北梦琐言 - *Beimeng Suoyan* (A Collection of Stories from the Tang Dynasty)
  • 玉谿编事 - *Yuxi Bianshi* (A Collection of Stories from the Tang Dynasty)

[3242]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 開運甲辰歲暮冬 - Classical time marker meaning 'in the winter of the year 甲辰 during the 开运 era.'
  • 中使 - Refers to a messenger or envoy sent by the emperor.
  • 宣問 - Means 'to summon and ask,' indicating a formal inquiry.
  • 玉盤中有一玉碗及一玉皆有碾文光荧可爱 - Detailed description of a dream vision, where a jade plate contains a jade bowl and a jade cup, all with intricate carvings that shine beautifully.
  • 吉梦 - Refers to a favorable dream, often interpreted as an omen.
  • 不敢有他占 - Means 'cannot be interpreted in any other way,' indicating that the dream must be seen as a positive sign.
  • 老而弥壮 - Means 'becoming even stronger with age,' indicating a positive aging process.
  • 心力不倦 - Means 'never tiring of the mind,' suggesting sustained mental vigor.
  • 年逾从心 - Means 'age surpassing the heart,' indicating advanced age but still vibrant.
  • 掌中草不脱 - Metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a symbol of longevity or immortality.
  • 内制草数年 - Refers to serving in the imperial court and drafting official documents for several years.
  • 仙方 - Refers to elixirs or methods for achieving immortality or longevity.
  • 騐矣 - Means 'proven to be true,' indicating that the methods have been verified.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋少主 - Refers to the young emperor of the Later Jin dynasty.
  • 青城山 - A significant Taoist site in Sichuan, known for its spiritual and cultural importance.
  • 皇姑 - A revered female figure, possibly a princess or a saintly woman.
  • 申天师怡神论 - A Daoist text discussing methods to enhance spiritual well-being and longevity.
  • 辛夤逊 - A scholar and official of the Shu kingdom, known for his literary works.
  • 孟蜀 - Refers to the Later Shu kingdom, a state during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 翰林学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official in the imperial court, responsible for drafting official documents and providing advice.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋少主 - The young emperor of the Later Jin dynasty.
  • 开运 - An era name of the Later Jin dynasty.
  • 甲辰 - A year in the sexagenary cycle.
  • 晋帝 - The emperor of the Later Jin dynasty.
  • 中使 - Imperial envoys.
  • 内署 - The inner office of the imperial court.
  • 李愼仪 - A scholar and official who responded to the emperor's inquiry.
  • 青城山 - A famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan.
  • 皇姑 - A revered female figure, possibly a princess or a saintly woman.
  • 辛夤逊 - A scholar and official of the Shu kingdom.
  • 孟蜀 - The Later Shu kingdom.
  • 翰林学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official in the imperial court.
  • 申天师 - A Daoist master.
  • 怡神论 - A Daoist text discussing methods to enhance spiritual well-being.
  • 仙方 - Elixirs or methods for achieving immortality or longevity.

[3243]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何致雍者贾人之子也 - Classical way to introduce someone's background, indicating they are the son of a merchant.
  • 幼而爽俊好学 - Describes the character's youthful qualities and academic inclination.
  • 尝从其叔父泊舟皖口 - Describes an event in the character's life; '泊舟' means to moor or anchor a boat, and '皖口' refers to the mouth of the Wan River.
  • 其叔夜梦一人官吏乘马从数仆来徃岸侧 - Describes a dream experienced by the character's uncle; '官吏' likely refers to an official or bureaucrat, and '来徃' means to come and go.
  • 复一人自后呼曰何仆射在此勿惊之 - Describes a second person in the dream; '何仆射' is a title, possibly referring to a high-ranking official, and '勿惊之' means 'do not be startled.'
  • 对曰诺不敢惊 - Describes the character's uncle's response to the dream; '诺' is an affirmative response, and '不敢惊' means 'will not be startled.'
  • 既寤徧访邻舟之人皆无姓何者 - Describes the character's uncle's actions after waking up from the dream; '既寤' means 'after waking up,' and '徧访' means 'searched thoroughly.'
  • 乃移舟入深浦中 - Describes the character's uncle's decision to move the boat; '深浦' refers to a deep harbor or anchorage.
  • 翌日大风涛所泊之舟皆没唯何氏存 - Describes the storm and its effects; '大风涛' means 'great storm,' and '所泊之舟' means 'the moored boats.'
  • 叔父乃谓致雍曰我家世贫贱吾复老矣何仆射必汝也善自爱 - Describes the character's uncle's advice to him; '善自爱' means 'take good care of yourself.'
  • 致雍后从知于湖南为节度判官 - Describes the character's later career; '节度判官' is a title for a high-ranking official in the military administration.
  • 致雍自谓当作相而居师长之任 - Describes the character's aspirations; '当作相' means 'would become a chancellor,' and '居师长之任' means 'hold a position of leadership.'
  • 后楚王希范嗣立复去帝号以致雍为节度判官检挍仆射竟卒于任 - Describes the character's final career and death; '嗣立' means 'succeeded,' '复去帝号' means 'again renounced the imperial title,' and '竟卒于任' means 'died in office.'
  • 【出稽神録】 - Indicates the source of the passage; '稽神録' is the title of the text from which the passage is taken.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 何致雍 - The name of the main character, a historical figure whose life is recounted in the passage.
  • 贾人之子 - Indicates that he is the son of a merchant, which was a common social status in ancient China.
  • 皖口 - The mouth of the Wan River, a specific geographical location in ancient China.
  • 楚王殷 - Refers to King Yin of Chu, a historical figure who proclaimed himself emperor.
  • 楚王希范 - Refers to King Xi Fan of Chu, who succeeded King Yin and again renounced the imperial title.
  • 翰林学士 - A scholar-official in the imperial academy, responsible for advising the emperor on literary and scholarly matters.
  • 节度判官 - A high-ranking official in the military administration, responsible for overseeing military affairs in a specific region.
  • 检挍仆射 - A title for a high-ranking official responsible for inspecting and overseeing various administrative functions.
  • 稽神録 - The title of the text from which the passage is taken, likely a historical or biographical record.

Proper Nouns

  • 何致雍 - The name of the main character.
  • 贾人之子 - Indicates that he is the son of a merchant.
  • 皖口 - The mouth of the Wan River.
  • 楚王殷 - King Yin of Chu.
  • 楚王希范 - King Xi Fan of Chu.
  • 翰林学士 - A scholar-official in the imperial academy.
  • 节度判官 - A high-ranking official in the military administration.
  • 检挍仆射 - A title for a high-ranking official responsible for inspecting and overseeing various administrative functions.
  • 稽神録 - The title of the text from which the passage is taken.

[3244]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甲寅岁 - A specific year in the Chinese calendar, translated as 'the year of Jia Yin'.
  • 沉痼 - Refers to a chronic or severe illness.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest or hermit.
  • 金花紫帔 - Refers to a golden flowered purple robe, a symbol of high rank in Taoism.
  • 从一小童 - Accompanied by a young boy or servant.
  • 色厉 - A stern or severe expression.
  • 既寤 - After waking up.
  • 甚则有之 - If it is so, then it is true.
  • 敛翅飞 - To fold one's wings and fly, metaphorically meaning to retreat or hide.
  • 吾道之宗正可依 - Our path's true essence can be relied upon.
  • 万物之先数在兹 - The number of all things is in this.
  • 不能行此欲何为 - If you cannot do this, what do you want to do?
  • 逊谢 - To apologize or show respect.
  • 色解 - The expression softened.
  • 索纸笔 - To ask for paper and ink.
  • 跪奉之 - To kneel and present it.
  • 飘风暴雨 - A strong wind and heavy rain, symbolizing a great disturbance.
  • 可思惟 - Can be thought about.
  • 望巢门 - Looking towards the nest door, possibly a metaphor for a place of refuge.
  • 流星堕地 - A shooting star falling to the ground.
  • 流星正尔耶 - Is the shooting star just like that?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伪吴 - Refers to the state of Wu during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 春坊吏 - An official in the imperial court, specifically in the spring office.
  • 冶城 - A city or place name, possibly referring to a specific location.
  • 稽神录 - A historical and supernatural record, a collection of stories about gods and spirits.
  • 鄂帅 - The commander of the army in E (a place name).
  • 颍州 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific city or region.
  • 流星 - A shooting star, often associated with omens or divine signs in Chinese culture.
  • 流星堕地 - A shooting star falling to the ground, often interpreted as a sign of good fortune or divine intervention.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭仁表 - A person's name.
  • 王璵 - A person's name.
  • 伪吴 - The state of Wu during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 春坊吏 - An official in the imperial court, specifically in the spring office.
  • 冶城 - A city or place name.
  • 稽神录 - A historical and supernatural record.
  • 鄂帅 - The commander of the army in E.
  • 颍州 - A place name.
  • 流星 - A shooting star.

[3245]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 进士 - A degree obtained through the imperial examination system, indicating a scholar-official.
  • 尝梦浴溪中 - A literal description of a dream where the subject bathes in a stream.
  • 吞此则明悟矣 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that consuming the pearls will lead to enlightenment or clarity.
  • 度其大者不可吞 - Indicates an assessment that the larger pearls cannot be swallowed.
  • 善为诗 - Means 'skilled in poetry,' indicating a talent for composing verse.
  • 分务广陵 - Means 'serving in Guangling,' indicating a position or duty in the city of Guangling.
  • 常病苦脾泄 - Describes a chronic condition of diarrhea affecting the spleen.
  • 困甚 - Means 'very distressed' or 'very troubled,' indicating severe discomfort.
  • 家人祷于后土祠 - Means 'the family prayed at the Hou Tu temple,' referring to a ritual act of seeking help from the Earth God.
  • 梦一妇人珠珥珠履 - Describes a dream where the subject sees a woman adorned with pearl earrings and shoes.
  • 衣五重皆编贝珠为之 - Means 'wearing five layers of clothing made entirely of cowrie beads,' indicating a luxurious and symbolic attire.
  • 此疾可治 - Means 'this disease can be treated,' indicating a diagnosis and prognosis.
  • 取青木香肉豆蔲等分枣肉为丸 - Describes a prescription for a medicinal formula, involving a mixture of ingredients.
  • 米饮下二十丸 - Means 'take twenty pills with rice wash,' indicating the method of administration.
  • 此药太疾平即止 - Means 'this medicine is too strong; stop when the illness is cured,' indicating the dosage and duration of treatment.
  • 如其言服之遂愈 - Means 'following his advice, he took it and recovered,' indicating the successful outcome of the treatment.
  • 累任大邑 - Means 'served in many large cities,' indicating a career of holding significant positions.
  • 假秩至评事 - Means 'reached the rank of Pingshi through a temporary appointment,' indicating a high-ranking position.
  • 世乱年老无复宦情 - Means 'in troubled times and old age, he had no more desire for officialdom,' indicating a change in attitude towards career.
  • 筑室于广陵法云寺之西 - Means 'built a house west of Fayun Temple in Guangling,' indicating a location for retirement.
  • 为终焉之计 - Means 'made plans for the end of his life,' indicating a decision to retire.
  • 束草加首 - Means 'a bundle of grass placed on the head,' indicating a symbolic gesture.
  • 口衔一刀 - Means 'holding a knife in the mouth,' indicating a specific action in the dream.
  • 两手各持一刀 - Means 'holding a knife in each hand,' indicating another specific action in the dream.
  • 入水而行 - Means 'walking into the water,' indicating the direction of movement in the dream.
  • 意甚异之 - Means 'found it very strange,' indicating the subject's reaction to the dream.
  • 俄而孙儒陷广陵 - Means 'soon after, Sun Ru captured Guangling,' indicating a historical event.
  • 儒部将李琼屯兵于法云寺 - Means 'Sun Ru's general Li Qiong stationed troops at Fayun Temple,' indicating a military action.
  • 恒止李令家 - Means 'always stayed at Li Ling's house,' indicating a frequent presence.
  • 父事令 - Means 'treated Li Ling like a father,' indicating a respectful relationship.
  • 及儒死 - Means 'after Sun Ru died,' indicating a change in circumstances.
  • 宣城裨蒋马殷刘建封辈率众南走 - Means 'the generals Jiang, Ma, Yin, Liu, and Jianfeng led their troops south,' indicating a military retreat.
  • 琼因彊令俱行 - Means 'Li Qiong then forced Li Ling to accompany him,' indicating a coercive action.
  • 及殷据湖南 - Means 'when Yin took control of Hunan,' indicating a shift in power.
  • 琼为桂管观察使 - Means 'Li Qiong became the Governor of Guilin,' indicating a new position.
  • 用令为荔浦令 - Means 'appointed Li Ling as the magistrate of Lipu,' indicating a promotion.
  • 则前梦之验也 - Means 'thus verifying the earlier dream,' indicating a connection between the dream and reality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 谢谔 - Refers to a scholar-official named Xie E.
  • 裴説 - Refers to Pei Shuo, a contemporary of Xie E.
  • 孙儒 - Refers to Sun Ru, a warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 蒋马殷刘建封 - Refers to Jiang, Ma, Yin, Liu, and Jianfeng, generals during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李琼 - Refers to Li Qiong, a general during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李令 - Refers to Li Ling, a scholar-official.
  • 后土祠 - Refers to the temple dedicated to the Earth God, a significant deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 广陵 - Refers to Guangling, an ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 法云寺 - Refers to Fayun Temple, a temple in Guangling.
  • 桂管 - Refers to Guilin, a region in Guangxi Province.
  • 荔浦 - Refers to Lipu, a county in Guangxi Province.

Proper Nouns

  • 谢谔 - A scholar-official.
  • 裴説 - A contemporary of Xie E.
  • 孙儒 - A warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 蒋马殷刘建封 - Generals during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李琼 - A general during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李令 - A scholar-official.
  • 后土祠 - A temple dedicated to the Earth God.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou.
  • 法云寺 - A temple in Guangling.
  • 桂管 - A region in Guangxi Province.
  • 荔浦 - A county in Guangxi Province.

[3246]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 梦吞日 - Dreamed of swallowing the sun; a symbolic or metaphorical expression in Classical Chinese, often associated with significant events or omens.
  • 非君所能当 - Not something you can handle; indicates that the dream's significance is beyond the individual's capacity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 广陵 - A historical region in present-day Jiangsu, known for its cultural and administrative importance.
  • 赤乌场 - A specific place or office, possibly named after the mythical bird '赤乌' (Red Bird), which is often associated with auspiciousness in Chinese mythology.
  • 稽神録 - A book or collection of stories and records, possibly containing supernatural or historical accounts.

Proper Nouns

  • 毛贞辅 - The name of the individual mentioned in the text, who had a significant dream.
  • 杨廷式 - The name of the official who interpreted the dream.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 梦吞日既寤腹犹 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous. It can be interpreted as 'after dreaming of swallowing the sun, his stomach still felt full' or 'after waking up from the dream, his stomach still felt full. The exact meaning depends on the context and the specific interpretation of '' (which is not a standard character and may be a typo or a rare character).

Idiomatic Expressions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '死见留山中孤魂飘泊不胜覊独' describes the spirit's state of being lost and lonely in the mountains.
  • The long sentence '夫人后还京我有兄名某见任京兆功曹可相为访令收己魂归于故乡深以相嘱言讫乃去' is elliptical and must be read as a sequence of actions and requests.
  • The complex sentence '昭成其夕宿白土具以梦问店者店人云贾明府妻坟去此六七里坠阬而死殡在山中已二年矣其言始末与梦相昭成深异之因记其事后奉入京寻其叚族具为説之叚氏举家悲泣遂令人徃取神柩之' describes the narrator's journey, the discovery of the truth, and the subsequent actions taken.
  • The long and complex sentence '扶沟令某霁者失其姓以大厯二年卒经半岁其妻梦与霁遇问其地下罪福霁曰吾生为进士陷于轻薄或毁讟词赋或诋诃人物今被地下所主每日送两虵及三蜈蚣出入七窍受诸痛苦不可堪忍法当三百六十日受此罪罪毕方得托生近以他事为阎罗王所剥旧裩狼藉为人所笑可作一裩与我妇云无物可作霁曰前者万年尉葢又将二绢来何得云无兼求铸像写法华经妇并许之然后方去尔' describes the spirit's punishment, the wife's dream, and the requests made by the spirit.

  • 三月诸发五月励受替遂尽室江陵而行 - In March, Zhi set out, and in May, Li took over and they all moved to Jiangling.

At noon, Zhi suddenly dreamed that Chen, with her hair loose, came to mourn and said, 'I am a lowly person from another place. Cui's lady had promised to stay with me forever, but how could I have been drowned in the Three Gorges while washing my hair, causing me to die and be buried in the bellies of fish and turtles? I cried so much that my clothes were wet.'
  • 俄而铿于东厢寐亦梦陈氏诉寃崔夫人不仁致我性命三峡铿与诸偶坐方讶其事其夜二人梦复如前铿甚慙谓诸曰某娘情性不当 - Soon after, Keng slept in the eastern room and also dreamed that Chen was complaining about Cui's lady's unkindness, which led to her death in the Three Gorges. Keng and Zhi were sitting together and were surprised by this. That night, both of them had the same dream again. Keng felt very ashamed and said to Zhi, 'My mother-in-law's nature is not right.'

  • 诜悸且甚因谓之曰某乆处外藩未得见天子向者朝对无乃不合于礼乎 - Describes the official's emotional state and his question, with '悸且甚' meaning 'very nervous,' '某乆处外藩' meaning 'I have been in the outer regions for a long time,' '未得见天子' meaning 'have not seen the emperor,' '向者朝对' meaning 'the previous audience,' and '无乃不合于礼乎' meaning 'perhaps it was not in accordance with etiquette?'

  • 时看汉月望汉天 - '时看' means 'often look at,' and '望' means 'to gaze at.'
  • 怨气冲云成彗孛 - '怨气' means 'anger,' '冲云' means 'to pierce the clouds,' and '彗孛' refers to a comet or a star that appears in the sky, often associated with bad omens.
  • 河湟咫尺不能收 - '河湟' refers to the Yellow River and the Huai River, and '咫尺' means 'very close,' while '不能收' means 'unable to reclaim.'

  • 使我心魂暗超忽 - '使我' means 'make me,' '心魂' means 'mind and spirit,' '暗' means 'secretly,' and '超忽' means 'suddenly and unexpectedly.'
  • 胜儿向邉塞弹征人血泪应阑干 - '胜儿' is a person's name, '向' means 'facing,' '邉塞' means 'border and frontier,' '弹' means 'to play,' '征人' means 'military personnel,' '血泪' means 'tears of blood,' and '应阑干' means 'responding to the tears on the railing.'
  • 歌既成刘生乘醉落茟草札而献王 - '歌既成' means 'the song was completed,' '刘生' is a person's name, '乘醉' means 'drunk,' '落茟草札' means 'to write on grass and bamboo,' and '献王' means 'presented to the king.'

  • 王之侍儿有不乐者 - '王之侍儿' means 'the king's attendants,' and '有不乐者' means 'some were unhappy.'

  • 王恃酒以金如意击胜儿首血淋襟袖 - '王恃酒' means 'the king relied on alcohol,' '金如意' means 'a golden scepter,' '击' means 'to strike,' '胜儿首' means 'the head of Sheng'er,' '血淋襟袖' means 'blood stained his clothes.'
  • 生乃惊起 - '生' means 'he,' and '惊起' means 'jumped up in surprise.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 美人忽夜夢被人邀去 - A beauty suddenly had a dream in which she was invited away. The phrase '被人邀去' is a classical way of describing being invited or taken by someone else, often with a sense of being led into an unknown or mysterious situation.
  • 縱酒宻防極歡而歸 - She drank freely, kept secrets, and returned extremely happy. '縱酒' refers to drinking freely or unrestrainedly, while '宻防' suggests keeping secrets or being discreet.
  • 歸辄流汗倦怠 - Upon returning, she would sweat and feel tired. '辄' indicates a habitual action, and '倦怠' means feeling exhausted or fatigued.
  • 此必术士所为也 - This must be the work of a magician or sorcerer. '术士' refers to a magician, sorcerer, or someone skilled in esoteric arts.
  • 但随宜以物识之 - Just mark it with something suitable to identify it. '随宜' means to do something in a suitable or appropriate manner, and '识之' means to mark or identify.
  • 果于东明观得其屏风手文尚在 - Indeed, at the East Bright Temple, the handprint on the screen was still there. '果' means 'indeed' or 'actually,' and '尚在' means 'still there' or 'remaining.'
  • 所居道士已遁矣 - The Taoist who lived there had already fled. '遁' means to flee or escape.
  • 东岩寺僧 - The monk of the East Rock Temple. '东岩寺' refers to the temple located on a rock or cliff in the east.

  • 郡有吕谊者 - There was a person named Lü Yi in the prefecture. '郡' refers to a prefecture or administrative region.
  • 遇简而厚币以遗意有所为 - He met Cui Jian and gave him a large sum of money, intending for him to do something. '厚币' means a large sum of money, and '遗意' means to have an intention or purpose.
  • 简问所欲 - Cui Jian asked what he wanted. '所欲' means 'what he wanted' or 'his desire.'
  • 继代有女未尝见人 - The daughter of the next generation had never been seen by anyone. '继代' refers to the next generation, and '未尝见人' means 'had never been seen by anyone.'
  • 闺帷之中一夕而失 - One night, she disappeared from her room. '闺帷' refers to a woman's private quarters, and '一夕而失' means 'disappeared one night.'
  • 蕴非常之术 - He possessed extraordinary skills. '蕴' means to possess or hold, and '非常之术' means extraordinary or unusual skills.
  • 愿知所捕 - He wishes to know where to capture her. '愿知' means 'wishes to know,' and '所捕' means 'where to capture.'
  • 瞑目无恨矣 - If he closes his eyes, he will have no regrets. '瞑目' means to close one's eyes, and '无恨' means 'no regrets.'
  • 易耳 - It is easy. '易' means easy, and '耳' is a particle indicating a light tone or emphasis.
  • 即于别室夜设几席焚名香以降神灵 - He immediately set up a table and chairs in a separate room, burned incense, and invoked the gods. '几席' refers to a table and chairs, '焚名香' means to burn incense, and '降神灵' means to invoke the gods.
  • 令吕生伏剑于户 - He ordered Lü to wait with a sword at the door. '伏剑' means to wait with a sword, and '于户' means 'at the door.'
  • 若胡僧来可执之 - If the foreign monk comes, you can capture him. '若' means 'if,' '胡僧' refers to a foreign monk, and '可执之' means 'you can capture him.'
  • 慎无伤也 - Be careful not to harm her. '慎' means careful, and '无伤' means 'not to harm.'
  • 书符呵之 - He wrote a talisman and commanded it. '书符' means to write a talisman, and '呵之' means to command or invoke.
  • 符飞出食顷间 - The talisman flew out in the blink of an eye. '食顷' means 'in the blink of an eye' or 'in a moment.'
  • 风声防树发屋 - The wind sounded like it was shaking the trees and lifting the roofs. '防树' means 'shaking the trees,' and '发屋' means 'lifting the roofs.'
  • 忽闻一甲卒进曰 - Suddenly, a soldier came forward and said. '忽闻' means 'suddenly heard,' '一甲卒' refers to a soldier, and '进曰' means 'came forward and said.'
  • 神兵备愿王所用 - The divine army is ready for the king's use. '神兵' means divine army, '备' means ready, and '愿王所用' means 'for the king's use.'
  • 主人某日失女可捕来 - The owner lost his daughter on a certain day, and you can capture her. '主人' means the owner, '某日' means a certain day, '失女' means lost his daughter, and '可捕来' means 'you can capture her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐开元中 - During the Tang Dynasty's Kaiyuan period. '开元' (Kaiyuan) was the era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a period known for its prosperity and cultural flourishing.

  • 屏风 - A screen or a partition. '屏风' refers to a decorative screen or partition used in traditional Chinese architecture.
  • 手文 - Handprint. '手文' refers to a handprint, often used in ancient texts to indicate a signature or a mark.
  • 东岩寺 - East Rock Temple. '东岩寺' is the name of a Buddhist temple, and '岩' (yan) means rock or cliff.

  • 吕谊 - Lü Yi. '吕谊' is the name of a person mentioned in the text.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 能抗后其大者之何复示众以不武也 - Complex phrase meaning 'If he can resist the great ones, why should he show the people that he is not brave?' The use of  (a variant of 乃) adds a layer of nuance, possibly indicating 'then' or 'thus.'
  • 既归令其党戒严 - 'After returning, he ordered his followers to be on high alert.' The term 戒严 (jiaiyan) means 'to be on high alert' or 'to enforce martial law.'
  • 是夜潞兵去元所据险三十里 - 'That night, the Lu army was thirty li away from Yuan's position.' The term 所据险 (suoju xian) means 'positioned at a strategic point.'
  • 见歩骑戈甲蔽山泽甚难之 - 'Seeing the infantry and cavalry with spears and armor covering the mountains and waters, he found it very difficult.' The term 蔽山泽 (bi shanze) means 'covering the mountains and waters,' and 甚难之 (shen nan zhi) means 'found it very difficult.'
  • 明方阵以前元领千余人直突之 - 'The next morning, he formed an array and led over a thousand people directly to attack them.' The term 明方阵 (ming fangzhen) means 'formed an array in the morning.'
  • 先胜后败酒酣被擒 - 'He initially won but later lost, and when he was drunk, he was captured.' The term 酒酣 (jiuhan) means 'drunk.'
  • 至上党絷之府狱严兵围守 - 'He was taken to Shangdang and imprisoned in the government prison, surrounded by strict guards.' The term 絷之府狱 (zhi zhi fuyu) means 'imprisoned in the government prison.'
  • 旦视枷穿中唯灯台耳 - 'In the morning, he saw that the枷 (jia) was broken, and only the灯台 (dengtai) remained.' The term 旦视 (dan shi) means 'in the morning, he saw.'
  • 失元所在夜分已逹铜鞮径诣神君谢罪 - 'He lost Yuan's location at midnight and reached Tongdi, directly going to the god to apologize.' The term 失元所在 (shi yuan suozai) means 'lost Yuan's location,' and 夜分 (yefen) means 'at midnight.'
  • 君怒曰庸奴终违我教今日虽幸而免斧锧亦行将及矣 - 'The god angrily said, 'You foolish servant, you finally disobeyed my teachings. Although you are fortunate to escape today, the executioner's axe will soon catch up with you.' The term 庸奴 (yongnu) means 'foolish servant,' and 斧锧 (fuzhi) means 'executioner's axe.'
  • 非吾徒也不顾而入郁悒趋出 - 'If you are not my disciple, you may go in, but you will feel depressed and rush out.' The term 非吾徒也 (fei wu tu ye) means 'if you are not my disciple,' and 郁悒 (yuyi) means 'depressed.'
  • 后复谒神君防心扣石石不为开矣而其术渐歇 - 'Later, he again visited the god, holding his heart and knocking on the stone, but the stone did not open. His magic gradually faded.' The term 防心 (fangxin) means 'holding his heart,' and 扣石 (koushi) means 'knocking on the stone.'
  • 犹为其党所説是秋率徒掠并州之大谷而并骑适至围之数重 - 'He was still persuaded by his followers, and in that autumn, he led his disciples to raid the Great Valley of Bingzhou, where the Bing cavalry happened to arrive and surrounded them several times.' The term 率徒 (shuai tu) means 'led his disciples,' and 适至 (shizhi) means 'happened to arrive.'
  • 术既不神遂斩之于阵其党与散归田里焉 - 'Since his magic was no longer effective, he was killed in battle, and his followers dispersed and returned to their fields.' The term 术既不神 (shu ji bu shen) means 'since his magic was no longer effective,' and 散归田里 (sanguan tianli) means 'dispersed and returned to their fields.'
  • 功徳山 - Mount Gongde, a place name.
  • 唐巢寇将乱中原汴中功徳山有妖僧逺近桑门皆归之 - 'During the Tang Dynasty, when the rebels were about to cause chaos in Central China, Mount Gongde in Bianzhong had an evil monk to whom all monks from far and near flocked.' The term 逺近桑门 (yuan jin sangmen) means 'monks from far and near.'
  • 至于士庶无不降附者 - 'Even commoners and officials were all subjugated.' The term 士庶 (shishu) means 'commoners and officials.'
  • 能于上画神寇放入人家令作祸祟幻惑居人通宵继昼不能安寝 - 'He could draw gods on paper and send them into people's homes, causing them to suffer and confusing the residents, making them unable to sleep all night and day.' The term 通宵继昼 (tongxiao jizhou) means 'all night and day.'
  • 或致人疾苦及命功徳山赠金作法则患立除之 - 'Sometimes he would cause people to suffer illness and hardship, and when they asked Mount Gongde to give gold to create a spell, their suffering would immediately be cured.' The term 赠金作法 (zengjin zuofa) means 'give gold to create a spell.'
  • 又画作甲兵夜夜于街坊嘶鸣腾践城郭天明即无所见 - 'He would also draw paper soldiers and horses that would neigh and trample the city walls every night, but by morning, they would be gone.' The term 踏践 (tajian) means 'trample.'
  • 又多画鸡犬焚祝之夜则鸣吠相咬囓于街衢居人不得安眠命而赠之即悄无影响 - 'He would also draw many chickens and dogs, burn them as offerings, and at night, they would bark and bite each other in the streets, making the residents unable to sleep. When people asked him to give gold, they would quietly disappear.' The term 悄无影响 (qiao wu yingxiang) means 'quietly disappear.'
  • 人既异其术趍术者愈众 - 'People were amazed by his magic, and those who followed his magic increased.' The term 趋术者 (qushu zhe) means 'those who followed his magic.'
  • 又滑州亦有一僧颇善祅术与功徳 - 'There was also a monk in Huazhou who was very good at magic, and he was associated with Mount Gongde.' The term 颇善祅术 (po shan yausu) means 'very good at magic.'

  • The sentence '明方阵以前元领千余人直突之' is compact, and the meaning must be inferred from context.
  • The sequence of events in '先胜后败酒酣被擒至上党絷之府狱严兵围守' must be carefully parsed to understand the sequence of events.

  • 唐姜抚先生不知何许人也 - Standard Classical Chinese phrase meaning 'the Mr. Jiang Fu of the Tang dynasty, whose origin is unknown.'
  • 尝着道士衣冠 - Literally 'once wore the robes and hat of a Taoist,' indicating a lifestyle or appearance.
  • 自云年已数百歳 - Literally 'claimed that he was already several hundred years old,' a hyperbolic statement.
  • 持符兼有长年之药度世之术 - Literally 'held talismans and possessed the elixir of longevity and the techniques to transcend the world,' indicating magical or alchemical abilities.
  • 时人谓之姜抚先生 - Literally 'people of the time called him Mr. Jiang Fu.'
  • 元宗皇帝高拱穆清栖神物表 - Formal description of the emperor's demeanor, meaning 'Emperor Yuanzong, who held high and serene, residing in a place of spiritual significance.'
  • 常有升仙之言 - Literally 'often spoke of ascending to immortality.'
  • 姜抚供奉别氶恩泽于诸州采药及修功徳 - Literally 'Jiang Fu was granted special favors by the emperor, traveling to various states to gather herbs and perform good deeds.'
  • 州县牧宰趋望风尘 - Literally 'governors and officials of the states and counties flocked to him like dust in the wind,' indicating widespread respect or adoration.
  • 学道者乞容立于门庭不能得也 - Literally 'those seeking the Dao begged to be allowed to stand at his door but could not,' indicating the difficulty in gaining his favor.
  • 荆岩者于太学四十年不第 - Literally 'Jing Yan, who had failed the imperial examinations for forty years in the Imperial Academy.'
  • 退居嵩少自称山人 - Literally 'retired to Mount Song and called himself a recluse,' indicating a lifestyle of seclusion and simplicity.
  • 颇通南北史知近代人物 - Literally 'quite knowledgeable about the histories of the Northern and Southern Dynasties and familiar with recent figures,' indicating a broad historical knowledge.
  • 尝谒抚抚简踞不为之动 - Literally 'once visited Jiang Fu, who remained aloof and unimpressed,' indicating Jiang Fu's haughty demeanor.
  • 先生年防何 - Literally 'how old is the Master?' - a question about Jiang Fu's age.
  • 公非信士何暇问年防 - Literally 'if you are not a sincere person, why bother asking about age?' - a rebuke to Jing Yan.
  • 先生既不能言甲子先生何朝人也 - Literally 'since you cannot speak of your age, which dynasty are you from?' - a direct challenge to Jiang Fu.
  • 梁朝人也 - Literally 'I am from the Liang dynasty,' a specific claim about Jiang Fu's origin.
  • 梁朝绝近先生亦非长年之人 - Literally 'the Liang dynasty was very recent, so you are not a long-lived person,' a logical challenge to Jiang Fu's claim of longevity.
  • 不审先生梁朝出仕为复隐居 - Literally 'I do not know whether you served as an official or lived as a recluse during the Liang dynasty,' a further probing question.
  • 吾为西梁州节度 - Literally 'I was the governor of Xiliangzhou,' a specific claim about Jiang Fu's position.
  • 何得诳妄上欺天子下惑世人 - Literally 'how can you lie and deceive the emperor above and mislead the people below,' a harsh rebuke to Jiang Fu.
  • 梁朝在江南何处得西梁州 - Literally 'the Liang dynasty was in the south, where did you get Xiliangzhou,' a logical challenge to Jiang Fu's claim.
  • 只有四平四安四镇四征将军何处得节度使 - Literally 'there were only four ranks of generals, how could there be a governor,' a further logical challenge.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 齊景公伐宋過泰山 - Literal meaning: 'Qi Jinggong attacked Song and passed through Mount Tai.' This phrase sets the stage for the narrative but requires understanding the historical context of Qi Jinggong's campaign and his visit to Mount Tai.
  • 夢見二人怒公恐 - Literal meaning: 'He dreamed of two people who were angry, which made him afraid.' This idiomatic expression describes a dream that caused fear, which is a common theme in classical literature.
  • 晏子以宋祖湯與伊尹爲言 - Literal meaning: 'Yanzi spoke about the ancestors of Song, Tang and Yi Yin.' This phrase requires understanding the historical figures Tang and Yi Yin, who are significant in Chinese history.
  • 其狀湯晢容多髭須伊尹黒而短 - Literal meaning: 'Their appearance was like Tang, with a bright face and many whiskers, and Yi Yin was black and short.' This description uses classical imagery to identify the figures in the dream.
  • 景公进军不听军鼓毁公恐乃散军不伐宋 - Literal meaning: 'Jinggong advanced his army, but the military drums were destroyed, which made him afraid, so he disbanded the army and did not attack Song.' This phrase describes a series of actions and their consequences.
  • 妬女泉有神庙泉瀵水深沈洁澈千丈 - Literal meaning: 'The Envy Girl Spring has a temple, and the spring water is deep, clear, and clean, reaching a thousand feet.' This phrase describes the physical characteristics of the spring and its temple.
  • 祭者投钱及羊骨皎然皆见 - Literal meaning: 'Those who worship throw coins and sheep bones, which are clearly visible.' This phrase describes the ritual practices at the temple.
  • 俗传妬女者介子推妹与兄竞去泉百里寒食不许断火至今犹然 - Literal meaning: 'It is said that the Envy Girl is the younger sister of Jie Zitui, who competed with her brother to leave the spring, a hundred miles away, and the Cold Food Festival does not allow the fire to be extinguished, which is still the case today.' This phrase requires understanding the historical figure Jie Zitui and the Cold Food Festival.
  • 伍子胥累谏吴王赐属镂劒而死 - Literal meaning: 'Wu Zixu repeatedly advised King of Wu, who granted him the Sword of Belonging and then died.' This phrase requires understanding the historical figure Wu Zixu and his relationship with King of Wu.
  • 悬吾首于南门以观越兵来 - Literal meaning: 'Hang my head at the South Gate to observe the coming of the Yue army.' This phrase describes a final act before death.
  • 鮧鱼皮裹吾尸投于江中 - Literal meaning: 'Wrap my body in carp skin and throw it into the river.' This phrase describes the method of burial.
  • 吾当朝暮乘潮以观吴之败 - Literal meaning: 'I will ride the tides morning and evening to observe the defeat of Wu.' This phrase describes a supernatural element.
  • 自是自海门山潮头汹高数百尺越钱塘渔浦方渐低小朝暮再来其声震怒雷奔电走百余里 - Literal meaning: 'Since then, the tide at the Sea Gate Mountain is surging and high, hundreds of feet, and the tide at the Qiantang Fish Harbor is gradually lowering and becoming smaller, coming back and forth every morning and evening, with a sound of thunder and lightning for hundreds of miles.' This phrase describes the natural phenomenon of the tides.
  • 时有见子胥乘素车白马在潮头之中 - Literal meaning: 'Sometimes people see Zixu riding a white horse in a white carriage in the middle of the tide.' This phrase describes a supernatural sighting.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 屈原以五月日投汨罗水而楚人哀之 - Describes the traditional story of Qu Yuan, a poet and statesman of the State of Chu, who drowned himself in the Miluo River on the fifth day of the fifth month of the lunar calendar. The phrase '哀之' means 'mourned him.'
  • 投水以祭之 - Means 'to throw food into the water to worship him.' Refers to the practice of throwing rice dumplings into the river to prevent fish from eating Qu Yuan's body.
  • 汉建武中 - Refers to the Jianwu period (25-57 AD) of the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 长沙区曲白日忽见一士人 - Means 'one day, Qu Qu of Changsha suddenly saw a scholar.' '白日' here means 'one day' or 'suddenly.'
  • 自云三闾大夫 - Means 'he said he was the Lord of the Three Clans.' '三闾大夫' was an official title held by Qu Yuan.
  • 闻君当见祭甚善 - Means 'I heard that you will worship me, which is very good.'
  • 但常年所遗恒为蛟龙所窃 - Means 'but usually the offerings are stolen by dragons.'
  • 此二物蛟龙所惮也 - Means 'these two things are what dragons fear.'
  • 皆汨罗水之遗风 - Means 'all these are remnants of the Miluo River.'
  • 李冰为蜀郡守 - Means 'Li Bing served as the governor of Shu Commandery.'
  • 入水戮蛟已为牛形 - Means 'he entered the water to kill the dragon and transformed into a cow.'
  • 江神龙跃冰不胜 - Means 'the river god transformed into a dragon and overpowered Li Bing.'
  • 吾前者为牛今江神必亦为牛矣 - Means 'I was a cow before, so now the river god must also be a cow.'
  • 以太白练自束以辨汝 - Means 'I will tie myself with a white silk ribbon to identify you.'
  • 吼呼而入 - Means 'he roared and entered.'
  • 须臾雷风大起天地一色 - Means 'soon after, a great storm arose, and the sky and earth became one color.'
  • 白武士乃齐射其神遂毙 - Means 'the white warriors shot at the river god, and he died.'
  • 从此蜀人不复为水所病 - Means 'from then on, the people of Shu were no longer troubled by water disasters.'
  • 至今大浪冲涛欲及公之祠皆弥弥而去 - Means 'until today, when large waves hit the temple, they always recede.'
  • 祠南数千家边江低圮虽甚秋潦亦不移 - Means 'south of the temple, thousands of households along the riverbank, even if the river rises in autumn, they are not affected.'
  • 适有石牛在庙庭下唐大和五年洪水惊溃 - Means 'there is a stone cow in the temple courtyard. In the fifth year of the Daitang period of the Tang Dynasty, a flood occurred, and the stone cow was damaged.'
  • 犹以白练为志水遂漂下左绵梓潼皆浮川溢峡伤数十郡唯西蜀无害 - Means 'still using the white silk ribbon as a sign, the water flowed down to Zuo, Mian, Zitong, and other places, causing floods in many prefectures, but only Shu was not affected.'

  • 峻坂下 - Refers to a steep hillside, emphasizing the difficult terrain.
  • 輦水將給行旅 - Means 'to pull water with a cart to supply travelers,' describing a laborious task.
  • 補履屩 - Means 'to mend shoes and sandals,' indicating a humble occupation.
  • 化爲書生 - Means 'to transform into a scholar,' suggesting a divine or supernatural intervention.
  • 何故不種菜以給 - Means 'why don't you grow vegetables to provide for yourself?'
  • 無種 - Means 'no seeds,' indicating a lack of resources.
  • 名家女 - Refers to a woman from a distinguished family.
  • 多求不許 - Means 'many requests were not granted,' indicating that the family was highly sought after.
  • 試求之 - Means 'to try to ask for her hand,' suggesting a tentative proposal.
  • 笑之以爲狂僻 - Means 'to laugh at him and consider him eccentric,' indicating initial skepticism.
  • 得白璧一雙錢百萬者與婚 - Means 'to marry him if he can bring a pair of white jade and a million coins,' setting a high condition for marriage.
  • 大愕 - Means 'greatly surprised,' indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 位至卿相 - Means 'to hold positions as high officials,' indicating significant political success.
  • 右北平諸陽其後也 - Means 'the various Yangs of Youbeiping are his descendants,' indicating the lineage.
  • 夜出屋後爲虎所取 - Means 'to be taken out of the house by a tiger at night,' indicating a supernatural event.
  • 十八日乃自還 - Means 'after eighteen days, he returned by himself,' indicating a prolonged absence.
  • 大知卜占無幽不驗 - Means 'he was very skilled in divination and could predict all hidden things,' indicating his exceptional abilities.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家甚缕 - Describes a family in dire poverty, where '缕' means extremely poor or destitute.
  • 拾流柴 - Literally means 'picking up floating firewood,' referring to collecting driftwood from the river.
  • 连船葢川而来 - Describes ships coming together across the river, possibly in a fleet or convoy.
  • 迳廻入浦 - Means 'directly turning into the inlet,' indicating a sudden change in direction.
  • 遣使往云 - Means 'sent a messenger to say,' introducing a message or communication.
  • 天女今当为徐郎妻 - A divine announcement that a heavenly maiden will become Xu Lang's wife.
  • 母兄妹劝励 - Refers to Xu Lang's mother, brother, and sister urging him to come out.
  • 浴水芬香 - Describes the water used for bathing as fragrant and unusual.
  • 缯绛之衣 - Refers to fine silk and red garments, likely gifts from the heavenly maiden.
  • 唯恐惧累膝床端 - Means 'only fearful and trembling, sitting at the edge of the bed,' indicating Xu Lang's nervousness.
  • 夜无醻接之礼 - Means 'there was no exchange of gifts at night,' suggesting a lack of formal interaction.
  • 遂懊叹卒 - Means 'he then grieved and died,' indicating a tragic end.
  • 全椒县 - Refers to a county in present-day Anhui Province, historically part of the state of Chu.
  • 丹阳丁氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Ding family in DanYang, a region in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 姑严酷 - Describes the woman's mother-in-law as harsh and cruel.
  • 作息不倦 - Means 'working tirelessly,' indicating the woman's diligence.
  • 自经死 - Means 'committed suicide by hanging,' indicating the method of her death.
  • 灵响闻于民间 - Means 'her spirit's voice was heard among the people,' suggesting a supernatural presence.
  • 发言于巫祝 - Means 'spoke through the medium of a shaman or priest,' indicating a communication channel.
  • 念人家妇女作息不倦 - Means 'remember the hardworking women,' suggesting a message of sympathy.
  • 使避九月七日 - Means 'to avoid the 7th day of the 9th month,' indicating a specific date to be avoided.
  • 勿用作 - Means 'do not work,' suggesting a prohibition.
  • 见形着缥衣戴青葢 - Means 'appearing in a light green robe with a blue canopy,' describing her appearance.
  • 从一婢至牛渚津求渡 - Means 'accompanied by a maid, she went to the crossing at Niu Zhi,' indicating her journey.
  • 两男子共乘船捕鱼 - Means 'two men were fishing together in a boat,' setting the scene.
  • 仍呼求载 - Means 'they called out for a ride,' indicating a request for assistance.
  • 笑共调弄之 - Means 'they laughed and teased her,' suggesting playful behavior.
  • 言听我为妇言当相渡也 - Means 'they said, 'If you agree to be our wife, we will take you across,' indicating a proposal.
  • 谓汝是佳人而无所知 - Means 'you are a beautiful woman but know nothing,' suggesting a critique.
  • 汝是人当使汝入泥死 - Means 'if you are a human, we will make you sink into the mud and die,' indicating a threat.
  • 是使汝入水便却入草中 - Means 'if you are a ghost, we will make you go into the water and then back into the grass,' indicating another threat.
  • 须臾有一老翁乘船载苇 - Means 'soon an old man came in a boat carrying reeds,' setting up the next encounter.
  • 妪从索渡 - Means 'the woman asked for a ride,' indicating a request.
  • 船 - Refers to the boat in the narrative.

  • 丹阳丁氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Ding family in DanYang, a region in present-day Jiangsu Province.

  • 吾是神非人也 - Direct statement: 'I am a ghost spirit, not a human.'
  • 自能得过 - Means 'I can pass through on my own.'
  • 宜使民间粗相闻知 - 'Should make the common people roughly known.'
  • 翁之厚意出苇相渡 - 'The old man's kind intention in offering reeds to help cross.'
  • 深有慙感 - Means 'deeply felt shame and gratitude.'
  • 当有以相谢者 - Means 'there will be something to repay you with.'
  • 媿燥湿不至 - 'Shameful that it doesn't reach the extremes of dryness and wetness.'
  • 何敢谢 - 'How dare I thank?'
  • 小姑可出戏 - 'The young wife can come out and play.'
  • 捉者觉重便是神来 - 'The one who catches feels heavy, then it is the spirit coming.'
  • 奠设酒果 - 'To set up offerings of wine and fruit.'
  • 貌辉辉有色 - 'Radiant appearance, with color.'
  • 跳不住 - 'Dancing continuously.'
  • 占众事卜行年蚕桑 - 'Divine various matters, divining the year, silkworm farming and mulberry cultivation.'
  • 又善射钩 - 'Also skilled in shooting hooks.'
  • 好则大儛恶便仰眠 - 'If good then large dance, if bad then lie on one's back.'
  • 躬试往捉 - 'Personally test and go to catch.'
  • 自跃穿屋 - 'Leaps on its own through the house.'
  • 永失所在 - Means 'lost forever.'

  • 庙有主簿 - The temple had a chief clerk or administrator.
  • 大福陈留蔡庸爲清河太守 - Cai Yong, a man from Chenliu with great fortune, served as the governor of Qinghe.
  • 过谒庙 - Visited the temple.
  • 有子名道亡已三十年 - His son named Dao had died thirty years ago.
  • 度朔君爲庸设酒 - Du Shuo Jun prepared wine for Cai Yong.
  • 贵子昔来欲相见 - Your son wished to meet you in the past.
  • 须臾子来 - Soon, the son arrived.
  • 度朔君自云父祖昔作兖州 - Du Shuo Jun mentioned that his ancestors were once in Yan State.
  • 有人士母病往祷 - There was a scholar whose mother was ill and went to pray.
  • 主簿云君逢天士留待 - The chief clerk said the deity met a heavenly scholar and stayed.
  • 闻西北有鼓声而君至 - Hearing the sound of drums from the northwest, the deity arrived.
  • 去复一人着白布单衣高冠冠似鱼头 - Another person came wearing a white robe and a high hat shaped like a fish head.
  • 谓君曰吾昔临庐山食白李忆之未久已三千岁 - He said, 'I once visited Mount Lu and ate white plums. It hasn't been long, but it's already been three thousand years.'
  • 日月易得使人怅然 - Time passes quickly, making one feel sad.
  • 君谓士曰先来南海君也 - The deity told the scholar, 'I came here from the South Sea.'
  • 士是书生君明通五经善礼记 - The scholar was a student who was well-versed in the Five Classics and the *Book of Rites*.
  • 与士论礼士不如也 - When discussing rituals, the scholar was not as knowledgeable.
  • 士乞救母病 - The scholar asked the deity to heal his mother's illness.
  • 君曰卿所居东有故桥人壊之 - The deity said, 'There is an old bridge to the east of where you live that has been damaged.'

[3403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行卿母犯之能复桥 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'if the mother of the official committed a crime and the bridge was destroyed' or 'the official's mother committed a crime and the bridge was rebuilt.' Context-dependent.
  • 便差曹公讨袁谭 - '便差' means 'immediately sent,' '曹公' refers to Cao Cao, and '讨袁谭' means 'to attack Yuan Tan.'
  • 使人从庙换千匹绢 - '使人' means 'sent someone,' '从庙' means 'from the temple,' and '换千匹绢' means 'to exchange for a thousand bolts of silk.'
  • 君不与曹公遣张郃毁庙 - '君不与' means 'you did not agree,' '曹公遣张郃' means 'Cao Cao sent Zhang He,' and '毁庙' means 'to destroy the temple.'
  • 未至百里君遣兵数万方道而来 - '未至百里' means 'not yet a hundred miles away,' '君遣兵数万' means 'you sent tens of thousands of troops,' and '方道而来' means 'arrived by road.'
  • 郃未达二里云雾绕郃军不知庙处 - '郃未达二里' means 'Zhang He had not yet reached two miles,' '云雾绕郃军' means 'clouds and mist surrounded Zhang He's army,' and '不知庙处' means 'did not know the location of the temple.'
  • 君语主簿曹公气盛宜避之 - '君语主簿' means 'you told the chief clerk,' '曹公气盛' means 'Cao Cao's anger was intense,' and '宜避之' means 'it is advisable to avoid him.'
  • 数日曹公猎得物大如麑大足色白如雪毛软滑可爱公以摩靣莫能名也 - '数日曹公猎得物大如麑' means 'several days later, Cao Cao hunted and caught an animal as large as a fawn,' '大足色白如雪' means 'with large feet and a color as white as snow,' '毛软滑可爱' means 'soft and smooth fur that was very lovable,' '公以摩靣莫能名也' means 'Cao Cao used it to rub his face, but could not name it.'
  • 夜闻楼上哭云小儿出行不还太祖拊掌曰此子言眞衰也晨将数百犬绕楼下犬得气衰突内外见有物大如驴自投楼下犬杀之庙神乃绝 - '夜闻楼上哭' means 'at night, they heard crying from upstairs,' '云小儿出行不还' means 'saying that the child had gone out and not returned,' '太祖拊掌曰此子言眞衰也' means 'Emperor Cao Cao clapped his hands and said, 'This child truly spoke falsely,' '晨将数百犬绕楼下' means 'in the morning, he led hundreds of dogs around the base of the building,' '犬得气衰突内外' means 'the dogs became weak and attacked inside and outside,' '见有物大如驴自投楼下' means 'they saw an object as large as a donkey fall from the top of the building,' '犬杀之庙神乃绝' means 'the dogs killed it, and the temple deity was extinguished.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曹公 - Refers to Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and politician during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 袁谭 - Refers to Yuan Tan, one of the sons of Yuan Shao, a rival of Cao Cao.
  • 张郃 - Refers to Zhang He, a general under Cao Cao.
  • 庙 - Refers to a temple or shrine, often associated with religious or ancestral worship.
  • 胡濶 - Refers to the north, often used in historical contexts to denote the region beyond the Great Wall.
  • 太祖 - A posthumous title given to Cao Cao, meaning 'Grand Ancestor.'

Proper Nouns

  • 曹公 - Cao Cao
  • 袁谭 - Yuan Tan
  • 张郃 - Zhang He
  • 庙 - Temple
  • 胡濶 - North
  • 太祖 - Grand Ancestor (posthumous title of Cao Cao)

[3404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嗜酒好色 - Common expression meaning 'loved drinking and women,' indicating a life of indulgence.
  • 挑挞无度 - Means 'teasing and provoking without restraint,' suggesting a habit of causing trouble.
  • 青骨死当为神 - Expression meaning 'if I die, I will become a god,' reflecting a belief in divine retribution or reward after death.
  • 解绶防之 - '解绶' means to untie the official seal, and '防之' means to bandage it. This implies that he was injured and sought medical help.
  • 有顷遂死 - Phrase indicating that he died shortly after being injured.
  • 立祠 - To build a temple or shrine in memory of someone.
  • 窃祀之 - To secretly worship or honor someone.
  • 啓祐 - To bless or protect.
  • 虫入人耳 - Superstition that small insects entering ears can cause illness or death.
  • 大火为灾 - Reference to a major fire disaster.
  • 将有大咎 - Indicates a severe misfortune or disaster.
  • 巫祝 - Refers to shamans or priests who communicate with the spirits.
  • 土地神 - Earth deity or local guardian spirit.
  • 下民 - Common people or subjects.
  • 立祠不尔 - If you do not build a shrine, there will be dire consequences.
  • 火及公宫 - Fire reached the royal palace, indicating a widespread disaster.
  • 议者 - Those who discussed or advised.
  • 以福尔下民 - To bring blessings to the common people.
  • 将又以大火为灾 - Another reference to a major fire disaster.

  • 蒋子文 - Historical figure from the Three Kingdoms period, known for his supernatural activities.
  • 广陵 - Ancient city in present-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 秣陵尉 - Low-ranking official in charge of law enforcement in Moling (present-day Nanjing).
  • 钟山 - Mountain in present-day Nanjing, known for its historical significance.
  • 吴先主 - Refers to Sun Quan, the founder of the state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 孙主 - Another reference to Sun Quan.
  • 小虫如鹿防 - Type of small insect, possibly a metaphor for a specific kind of bug that was believed to cause harm.

[3405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 爲厲宜有以抚之 - To pacify the calamity, there should be a way to comfort it.
  • 使使者封子文爲中都侯 - Sent envoys to confer the title of Zhongdu Marquis on Zi Wen.
  • 次弟子绪爲长水校尉 - Next, his younger brother Zi Xu was appointed as the Longshui Garrison Commander.
  • 皆加印绶 - All were given seals and ribbons.
  • 爲庙堂转号钟山爲蒋山 - In the temple hall, the name of Zhongshan was changed to Jiangshan.
  • 今建康东北蒋山是也 - Now known as Jiangshan in the northeast of Jiankang.
  • 自是灾厉止息 - From then on, the calamities ceased.
  • 百姓遂大事之 - The common people then greatly revered it.
  • 陳郡謝玉爲琅邪内史 - Xie Yu of Chen Prefecture became the Governor of Langya.
  • 在京城 - In the capital city.
  • 其年虎暴杀人甚众 - That year, tigers attacked and killed many people.
  • 有一人以小船载年少妇以大刀揷着船浃暮来至逻将出语云 - There was a man who used a small boat to carry a young woman, with a large knife stuck in the boat, arriving at the patrol post in the evening and speaking to the patrol leader.
  • 此间顷来甚多草秽君载细小作此轻行大爲不易可止逻宿也 - There have been many weeds here recently; your load is small, making this light journey difficult; you should stay at the patrol post overnight.
  • 相问讯既毕逻将适还去其妇上岸便爲虎取去 - After exchanging greetings, the patrol leader just happened to return, and as the woman went ashore, she was taken by the tiger.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蚕室 - A room or space dedicated to raising silkworms, symbolizing the importance of silk production in ancient Chinese society.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '张诚之夜见一妇人立于宅东南角举手招诚诚就之' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'On a night, Zhang Cheng saw a woman standing at the southeast corner of his house, raising her hand to beckon him; he approached her.'
  • The woman's speech '妇人曰此地是君家蚕室我即是地之神' is a single, uninterrupted statement that must be read as a complete thought: 'This place is your family's silk room; I am the spirit of this place.'
  • The long sentence '明年正月半宜作白粥泛膏于上以祭我当令君蚕桑百倍言绝失之' must be read as a single, conditional statement: 'Next year on the 15th of the first lunar month, you should make white porridge with fat spread on top to offer to me; I will make your mulberry cultivation and silkworm rearing a hundred times more prosperous; after she finished speaking, she vanished.'
  • The sentence '如言爲作膏粥自此年年大得蚕世人正月半作膏粥由此故也' is also elliptical and must be read as a single, explanatory statement: 'As she said, he made the fat porridge; from then on, year after year, he had a great harvest of silkworms; people make fat porridge on the 15th of the first lunar month, and this is the reason for the custom.'

Proper Nouns

  • 張誠之 - The name of the protagonist, Zhang Cheng, who experiences the supernatural encounter.
  • 續齊諧記 - The title of the collection of stories in which this tale is found.

  • 余枝在荐下可与人使着出入辟恶灾 - Complex archaic expression; '余枝' likely refers to a branch or part of something, '荐下' means 'under the mat' or 'in the house,' '可与人使着' suggests 'can be used by people,' '出入' means 'going in and out,' and '辟恶灾' means 'to ward off evil and disasters.' Together, it means 'a branch under the house that can be used by people to go in and out and ward off evil and disasters.'
  • 因道曰 - Means 'because he said' or 'as he said,' introducing a quotation.
  • 王甲李乙吾皆与之 - '王甲李乙' are proper names, likely referring to two individuals. '吾皆与之' means 'I have all of them.'
  • 遂执祐手与辞 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thereupon,' '执祐手' means 'held Yu's hand,' and '与辞' means 'to say goodbye' or 'to part ways.' Together, it means 'then held Yu's hand and said goodbye.'
  • 时祐得安眠夜中忽觉忽呼左右令开被神以水灌我将大沾濡 - '时祐得安眠' means 'at the time Yu was sleeping peacefully,' '夜中忽觉' means 'suddenly woke up in the middle of the night,' '忽呼左右' means 'immediately called the attendants,' '令开被' means 'ordered to open the blanket,' '神以水灌我' means 'a divine being poured water on me,' and '将大沾濡' means 'was about to be drenched.' Together, it means 'at the time Yu was sleeping peacefully, in the middle of the night he suddenly woke up and immediately called the attendants, ordering them to open the blanket; a divine being poured water on me, was about to be drenched.'
  • 开被而信有水在上被之下下被之上不浸如露之在荷量之得三升七合 - '开被而信有水在上被之下' means 'when the blanket was opened, there was indeed water under the top blanket,' '下被之上' means 'on top of the bottom blanket,' '不浸如露之在荷' means 'not soaking like dew on a lotus leaf,' and '量之得三升七合' means 'measured it to be three sheng and seven he.' Together, it means 'when the blanket was opened, there was indeed water under the top blanket, on top of the bottom blanket, not soaking like dew on a lotus leaf, measured it to be three sheng and seven he.'
  • 于是疾三分愈二数日大除 - '于是' means 'thus,' '疾' means 'illness,' '三分愈二' means 'two-thirds better,' '数日' means 'several days,' and '大除' means 'greatly relieved.' Together, it means 'thus, his illness improved by two-thirds, and after several days, it was greatly relieved.'
  • 凡其所道当取者皆死亡唯王文英半年后乃亡所道与赤笔人皆经疾病及兵乱皆亦无恙 - '凡其所道当取者' means 'all those he mentioned should be taken,' '皆死亡' means 'all died,' '唯王文英半年后乃亡' means 'only Wang Wenyi died half a year later,' '所道与赤笔人' means 'those he mentioned and the red-pen people,' '皆经疾病及兵乱' means 'all went through diseases and wars,' and '皆亦无恙' means 'all were unharmed.' Together, it means 'all those he mentioned should be taken, all died, only Wang Wenyi died half a year later; those he mentioned and the red-pen people all went through diseases and wars, all were unharmed.'
  • 温峤平苏峻之难及于湓口乃试照焉果见官寺赫奕人徒甚盛又见群小儿两两为偶乘轺车驾以黄羊睢盱可恶温即梦见神怒曰当令君知之乃得病也 - '温峤平苏峻之难' means 'Wen Qiao pacified the difficulties caused by Su Jun,' '及于湓口' means 'and at the port of Pun,' '乃试照焉' means 'then tried to illuminate it,' '果见官寺赫奕人徒甚盛' means 'and indeed saw official temples shining brightly with many people,' '又见群小儿两两为偶乘轺车驾以黄羊' means 'and also saw groups of children paired up riding in light carriages with yellow sheep,' '睢盱可恶' means 'looking around with a hateful expression,' '温即梦见神怒曰当令君知之' means 'Wen immediately dreamed of a divine being saying, 'I will make you know,' '乃得病也' means 'and then fell ill.' Together, it means 'Wen Qiao pacified the difficulties caused by Su Jun and at the port of Pun, then tried to illuminate it, and indeed saw official temples shining brightly with many people, and also saw groups of children paired up riding in light carriages with yellow sheep, looking around with a hateful expression; Wen immediately dreamed of a divine being saying, 'I will make you know,' and then fell ill.'

  • 取三十人遣吏送苗穷山林中鏁腰系树 - Means 'they took thirty people and sent Huang Miao to a remote mountain forest, locking his waist and tying him to a tree,' describing the punishment.

  • 县令呼苗具疏事覆前后所取人遍问其家并符合焉髀爲防所伤创瘢尚在 - Means 'the county magistrate called Huang Miao and asked him to list the details of the people he had taken, and then asked each family. All the details matched, and the wounds on his thighs, inflicted by the bindings, were still visible,' verifying the accuracy of the records.
